question_text: Given $\Phi ( 1 . 6 4 5 ) = 0 . 9 5$ and $\Phi ( 2 . 3 3 ) = 0 . 9 9$ . For a random sample $X _ { 1 } , . . . , X _ { n } \sim N ( \mu , 2 ^ { 2 } )$ with $\mu$ unknown, test $H _ { 0 } : \mu = 1 0$ vs $H _ { 1 } : \mu = 1 2$ using a rule that rejects $H _ { 0 }$ when $\overline { { X } }$ is large. Find the smallest n (in integer) so that Type I error is 0.05 and Type II error is at most 0.01.
solution:
Under $H _ { 0 } , { \overline { { X } } } \sim N ( 1 0 , 4 / n )$ . The level-0.05 critical value c satisfies

 $ P _ { H _ { 0 } } ( \overline { { { X } } } > c ) = 0 . 0 5 \iff \frac { \overline { { { X } } } - 1 0 } { 2 / \sqrt { n } } > 1 . 6 4 5 \implies c = 1 0 + \frac { 2 \cdot 1 . 6 4 5 } { \sqrt { n } } . $ 

Power at $\mu = 1 2$ is

 $ P _ { 1 2 } ( \overline { { { X } } } > c ) = P \bigg ( \frac { \overline { { { X } } } - 1 2 } { 2 / \sqrt { n } } > \frac { c - 1 2 } { 2 / \sqrt { n } } \bigg ) = P \big ( Z > - \sqrt { n } + 1 . 6 4 5 \big ) . $ 

Type II error $\leq 0 . 0 1$ means $P _ { 1 2 } ( \overline { { X } } > c ) \geq 0 . 9 9 , \mathrm { i } . 6$ .

 $ \Phi ( { \sqrt { n } } - 1 . 6 4 5 ) \geq 0 . 9 9 \implies { \sqrt { n } } - 1 . 6 4 5 \geq 2 . 3 3 \implies { \sqrt { n } } \geq 3 . 9 7 5 . $ 

Hence $n \geq 1 5 . 8 0$ , so the smallest integer is 16 .
Quick Tip

For known variance $\sigma ^ { 2 }$ in one–sided tests, solve the size constraint for the critical value, then impose the desired power under the alternative using the z–transform.
